 {"id":"bd-3p04","title":"Add wa robot approve command","description":"# wa robot approve

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-approve.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Integrate with ApprovalStore to validate and consume tokens.

## Implementation
1. Accept approval code parameter
2. Query ApprovalStore for token
3. Validate workspace scope, expiry, fingerprint
4. Mark as consumed on success
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Valid code consumes token and returns success
- Unit: Expired codes return E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED
- Unit: Already-consumed codes return E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED
- Unit: Wrong pane/workspace returns appropriate error
- E2E: Full approval flow (request → grant → consume)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validates approval codes via ApprovalStore
- [ ] Consumes token on success
- [ ] All error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] Audit trail records approval consumption
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

Related: bd-39wn (detailed implementation spec)

 {"id":"bd-3rwt","title":"Add wa robot workflow list command","description":"# wa robot workflow list

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-list.json)
❌ Implementation uses hardcoded workflow list

## What Remains
Query WorkflowEngine registry instead of hardcoded list.

## Implementation
1. Access WorkflowEngine from command context
2. Call `engine.list_workflows()` or equivalent
3. Map to response format with name, description, triggers, parameters

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: List reflects registered workflows (not hardcoded)
- Unit: Shows enabled/disabled state correctly
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for comprehensive scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists workflows from WorkflowEngine (not hardcoded)
- [ ] Shows enabled/disabled state
- [ ] Includes trigger event types
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"wa-upg.2.1","title":"Design: ActionPlan/StepPlan schema + hashing + rendering","description":"# Task: Design ActionPlan/StepPlan schema + hashing

## Goal
Specify the concrete types and serialization/hashing rules for ActionPlan/StepPlan so they are:
- stable/deterministic
- safe to approve (hash-bound)
- easy to render for humans and machines

## Requirements
- Define Rust types (or equivalent) for:
  - `ActionPlan`
  - `StepPlan`
  - `Precondition`
  - `Verification`
  - `OnFailure` / `Fallback`
  - `IdempotencyKey`
- Define canonical serialization:
  - stable field ordering
  - stable list ordering
  - explicit versioning (`plan_version`)
- Define plan hash derivation:
  - hash the canonical serialization
  - include workspace scope and relevant environment invariants
  - avoid including wall-clock timestamps in the hash
- Rendering:
  - TTY: progressive disclosure (summary + expand details)
  - JSON: fully structured (no lossy strings)

## Testing
- Provide fixture plans in tests to validate hash stability across runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The plan format is unambiguous and implementable.
- Hash derivation is deterministic and excludes non-deterministic inputs.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:36:33.798266048Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:51:16.061976042Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:51:16.061845606Z","close_reason":"Created design document at docs/action-plan-schema.md with:
- Rust type definitions for ActionPlan, StepPlan, Precondition, Verification, OnFailure, IdempotencyKey
- Canonical serialization spec (stable field ordering, plan_version field, excluded fields)
- Deterministic plan hash derivation algorithm (sha256, canonical string format)
- Rendering specs for TTY (progressive disclosure), JSON (fully structured), TOON (token-optimized)
- Integration points mapping to existing code (DryRunReport, PlannedAction, workflows::WaitCondition)
- Migration path and future considerations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-design","area-tests","area-workflows","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:36:36.455084227Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.2.2","title":"Implement ActionPlan/StepPlan core types + canonical serialization","description":"# Task: Implement ActionPlan/StepPlan core

## Goal
Implement the ActionPlan/StepPlan types in a shared core module and make them usable by workflows, dry-run previews, and approvals.

## Requirements
- Implement types and builder APIs:
  - ergonomic constructors
  - no footguns (avoid partial/invalid plans)
- Canonical serialization:
  - stable JSON encoding (for hashing)
  - stable pretty-print (for human readability)
- Plan hashing:
  - compute hash from canonical encoding
  - expose `plan_hash` as a first-class field
- Validation:
  - validate invariants (step IDs unique, idempotency keys present where required)

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - deterministic serialization and hashing
  - validation errors are actionable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A plan can be constructed, validated, serialized, hashed, and rendered deterministically.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:36:43.322453491Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:02:56.903668992Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:02:56.903241426Z","close_reason":"Implemented ActionPlan/StepPlan core types in crates/wa-core/src/plan.rs:
- ActionPlan: complete plan with metadata, steps, preconditions, on_failure
- StepPlan: individual step with action, verification, failure handling
- StepAction: tagged enum for all action types (SendText, WaitFor, AcquireLock, etc.)
- Precondition: pre-execution checks (PaneExists, PatternMatched, LockHeld, etc.)
- Verification: post-execution validation strategies
- OnFailure: failure handling (Abort, Retry, Skip, Fallback, RequireApproval)
- IdempotencyKey: content-addressed step identifier
- PlanId: content-addressed plan identifier
- ActionPlanBuilder: ergonomic plan construction
- Canonical serialization for deterministic hashing
- compute_hash() method using SHA-256
- validate() method for plan validation
- 7 unit tests passing: hash determinism, content changes, validation, JSON roundtrip","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-safety","area-tests","area-workflows","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:36:49.188988535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.2.3","title":"Integrate workflow runner to emit/execute StepPlans (plan-first execution)","description":"# Task: Plan-first workflow execution

## Goal
Refactor workflow execution to be plan-first:
- generate StepPlans up front when possible
- execute with per-step verification + durable logging

## Requirements
- Workflow runner behavior:
  - construct an ActionPlan (StepPlans) prior to execution (or incrementally with explicit boundaries)
  - persist the plan before performing side effects
  - execute steps with:
    - precondition checks
    - PolicyEngine authorization
    - verification
    - bounded retries
    - explicit failure handling
- Step log model:
  - record start/end timestamps (monotonic where possible)
  - record verification evidence (snippets, state queries)
  - record policy decisions (redacted)

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - fixture workflow generates expected plan + step logs
  - failure in step N produces actionable error referencing step id/kind

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow runs are explainable via their persisted ActionPlan and step logs.
- Retrying a workflow does not double-apply side effects when idempotency keys are used.

-{"id":"wa-upg.2.4","title":"Persist ActionPlans + step logs; expose via CLI/robot for explainability","description":"# Task: Persist ActionPlans and step logs

## Goal
Make ActionPlans and per-step execution logs durable and queryable for explainability, debugging, and replay.

## Requirements
- Storage:
  - persist ActionPlan (canonical JSON + hash) linked to workflow execution
  - persist step logs with:
    - step id/kind
    - policy decision summary
    - verification evidence references
    - errors with stable codes
- Query surfaces:
  - robot/MCP: fetch plan and step logs by workflow id
  - human CLI: view plan/logs with progressive disclosure

## Testing
- Storage integration tests:
  - insert plan + step logs and query roundtrip
  - schema migration stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a workflow execution id, wa can show:
  - the ActionPlan that was executed
  - the step-by-step results and evidence

 {"id":"wa-upg.2.5","title":"Tests: plan determinism, hashing, idempotency, and failure reporting","description":"# Task: Tests for ActionPlan/StepPlan

## Goal
Ensure ActionPlan/StepPlan are deterministic and safe to use as the core substrate for approvals and execution.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - canonical serialization is stable
  - plan hash stability across runs/platforms
  - plan validation catches invalid/incomplete plans
  - idempotency keys prevent double-apply on retry
- Integration tests:
  - workflow runner emits plan + logs; restart/resume preserves invariants

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover determinism, idempotency, and error reporting in a way that prevents regressions.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:16.924964213Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:29:28.218186511Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:29:28.218130716Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive test suite for ActionPlan/StepPlan (28 tests total):
- Hash stability tests: known value verification, excludes timestamps/metadata, includes workspace/title
- Idempotency key tests: differs by workspace, step_number, and action
- Validation tests: duplicate step IDs, unknown step references, invalid step numbers
- Canonical serialization tests: stability for Precondition, Verification, OnFailure, StepAction, WaitCondition
- Integration tests: complex plan with all features, JSON roundtrip, helper methods
All tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-tests","area-workflows","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:21.587865574Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.2.6","title":"E2E: plan preview + workflow execution logs (no sleeps, rich artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E plan preview + workflow execution logs

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that ActionPlan preview and execution logging work in a realistic run.

## Scenario
- Trigger a known workflow.
- Capture the ActionPlan preview (dry-run / prepare).
- Execute and verify:
  - plan is persisted
  - step logs exist and are queryable
  - failures (if induced) reference specific step boundaries

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - plan JSON
  - step logs JSON
  - concise human summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E produces deterministic plan and step logs.
- Artifacts are sufficient to debug without re-running interactively.

 {"id":"wa-upg.2.7","title":"Docs: ActionPlan/StepPlan mental model + extension guidance","description":"# Task: Document ActionPlan/StepPlan

## Goal
Make the plan-first execution model understandable to future contributors and power users.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - what ActionPlans and StepPlans are
  - how verification and idempotency work
  - how plan hashing relates to approvals
  - how to add a new Step kind safely

## Acceptance Criteria
- A contributor can implement a new workflow step without inventing ad-hoc behavior.
